name: Olamide Zaccheaus
headline: Falcons reunion can't outrun a sliding profile
body:
Zaccheaus took a clear step back in 2025, finishing WR89 on 65 targets and a 12.2% share across 14 games in Chicago. His efficiency cratered: a minus-0.044 EPA per play, a 44.6% success rate, and just 1.0 yards per route run after posting 1.9 the year prior. The 8.0-yard average per catch and 313 receiving yards tell the story of a low-leverage role, even with 12 red-zone looks producing only two scores. The juice he flashed in 2024 (0.392 EPA, 56.2% success) simply didn't carry over.

Now back in Atlanta, he profiles as depth behind Drake London with rookie competition pushing for the same slot snaps. Mooney's exit opens a sliver of volume, but a 0.207 target-per-route rate and shrinking air-yards share cap the ceiling. He's a late-round dart and waiver watch, nothing more, until the route share actually expands.
